items.title: Model parameters as typed values
items.description: Generation settings are ordinary typed variables: temperature and top_p are floats, max_tokens and seed are ints, and stream is a bool. Using the wrong type (e.g. "512" instead of 512) makes the request invalid.

items.title: Chat history as list[dict]
items.description: A conversation is a list of message dicts, each shaped like {"role": "user", "content": "..."}. Because lists and dicts are mutable, you append new turns in place as the chat grows.

items.title: Embeddings as numeric lists
items.description: An embedding is a vector — a list (or NumPy array) of floats. Treating it as the right type lets you compute similarity, store it in a vector database and feed it to models.

items.title: Config and secrets as strings
items.description: Model names, API endpoints and API keys are strings, usually read from environment variables. Keeping them as well-named string variables avoids hard-coding and typos.

items.title: Token accounting with ints
items.description: Prompt and completion token counts are integers used to estimate cost and to stay within the model's context window. Integer math here directly affects billing and truncation.
